test.name: Scenario 1: User Request -> Admin Approve -> User Download

kw.name: Create Session
kw.msg: Creating Session using : alias=api, url=https://deploy-production-88fa.up.railway.app/api, headers={}, cookies={}, auth=None, timeout=None, proxies=None, verify=False, debug=0
kw.doc: Create Session: create a HTTP session to a server

kw.name: Login
Create Dictionary
kw.msg: ${body} = {'username': 'exportTester1', 'password': '123456789'}
kw.doc: Creates and returns a dictionary based on the given ``items``.

kw.name: POST On Session
kw.msg: POST Request : url=https://deploy-production-88fa.up.railway.app/api/auth/login path_url=/api/auth/login headers={'User-Agent': 'python-requests/2.32.3', 'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, deflate, br', 'Accept': '*/*', 'Connection': 'keep-alive', 'Content-Length': '54', 'Content-Type': 'application/json'} body=b'{"username": "exportTester1", "password": "123456789"}'

kw.name: Create Dictionary

kw.doc: Makes a variable available everywhere within the scope of the current suite.

${dl} = <Response [200]>
kw.doc: Sends a GET request on a previously created HTTP Session.

kw.name: Status Should Be
kw.doc: Fails if response status code is different than the expected.

kw.name: Login

${dl} = <Response [200]>
kw.doc: Sends a GET request on a previously created HTTP Session.

kw.name: Status Should Be
kw.doc: Fails if response status code is different than the expected.
